verses.text: Herod kills James, the brother of John, with the sword. A martyr falls. The first apostle gives his life for the faith.
Herod kills James, the brother of John, with the sword. A martyr falls. The first apostle gives his life for the faith.
He imprisons Peter, but 'prayer was made without ceasing of the church.' Earnest prayer. The church meets the threat on its knees.
He imprisons Peter, but 'prayer was made without ceasing of the church.' Earnest prayer. The church meets the threat on its knees.
He imprisons Peter, but 'prayer was made without ceasing of the church.' Earnest prayer. The church meets the threat on its knees.
An angel wakes Peter, his chains fall off, 'Gird thyself, and follow me.' Sudden rescue. God frees His servant in the dead of night.
An angel wakes Peter, his chains fall off, 'Gird thyself, and follow me.' Sudden rescue. God frees His servant in the dead of night.
An angel wakes Peter, his chains fall off, 'Gird thyself, and follow me.' Sudden rescue. God frees His servant in the dead of night.
Past the gates, the iron gate opens of itself; 'now I know... the Lord hath sent his angel.' Real deliverance. He wakes to find his rescue is no dream.
Past the gates, the iron gate opens of itself; 'now I know... the Lord hath sent his angel.' Real deliverance. He wakes to find his rescue is no dream.
Past the gates, the iron gate opens of itself; 'now I know... the Lord hath sent his angel.' Real deliverance. He wakes to find his rescue is no dream.
He comes to the praying house; Rhoda, knowing his voice, leaves him at the gate for gladness. Joyful haste. Surprise overwhelms her sense.
He comes to the praying house; Rhoda, knowing his voice, leaves him at the gate for gladness. Joyful haste. Surprise overwhelms her sense.
He comes to the praying house; Rhoda, knowing his voice, leaves him at the gate for gladness. Joyful haste. Surprise overwhelms her sense.
They say she is mad; Peter tells how the Lord brought him out, and departs. Answered prayer. The God they prayed to has done it.
They say she is mad; Peter tells how the Lord brought him out, and departs. Answered prayer. The God they prayed to has done it.
They say she is mad; Peter tells how the Lord brought him out, and departs. Answered prayer. The God they prayed to has done it.
No small stir; Herod seeks him and slays the keepers. Frustrated rage. The tyrant cannot find the man God has freed.
No small stir; Herod seeks him and slays the keepers. Frustrated rage. The tyrant cannot find the man God has freed.
Herod is hailed, 'It is... a god'; he gives not God the glory and is 'eaten of worms.' Pride judged. God strikes the man who steals His honour.
Herod is hailed, 'It is... a god'; he gives not God the glory and is 'eaten of worms.' Pride judged. God strikes the man who steals His honour.
Herod is hailed, 'It is... a god'; he gives not God the glory and is 'eaten of worms.' Pride judged. God strikes the man who steals His honour.
Herod is hailed, 'It is... a god'; he gives not God the glory and is 'eaten of worms.' Pride judged. God strikes the man who steals His honour.
But 'the word of God grew and multiplied'; Barnabas and Saul return with John Mark. Gospel triumphs. The word advances over its enemies.
But 'the word of God grew and multiplied'; Barnabas and Saul return with John Mark. Gospel triumphs. The word advances over its enemies.
